skill.name: caveman-compress
skill.description: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
skill.summary: Compress markdown memory files into concise caveman format to reduce AI session token costs by approximately 46 percent while preserving all technical content.
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 107 potential issues across 10 files with a risk score of 100/100. After semantic evaluation, all flagged patterns are false positives. The external_commands findings (74 locations) are primarily markdown backticks in documentation files misidentified as shell execution. The one real subprocess call in scripts/compress.py uses hardcoded arguments with stdin input and no shell interpolation. Network findings (2 locations) are static URLs in README.md documentation, not runtime requests. Filesystem findings (2 locations) are standard path resolution for a file-processing tool. Environment access findings (9 locations) are legitimate ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and CAVEMAN_MODEL configuration for API usage. The critical heuristic finding about code execution plus network plus credential access is a false positive describing the normal operation of an Anthropic API client. No prompt injection attempts, data exfiltration patterns, or malicious intent detected. The tool has appropriate safeguards including file size limits (500KB), automatic backups, and retry logic with rollback on failure.

low_findings.title: Subprocess CLI Call
low_findings.description: The tool calls the claude CLI via subprocess.run as a fallback when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set. Arguments are hardcoded and user content is passed via stdin, not shell arguments. This is a standard pattern for Claude CLI integration. The risk is minimal but warrants documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of subprocess.run with hardcoded arguments and stdin input - standard CLI integration pattern, no shell=True or user-controlled argument injection.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: The tool reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from environment variables to authenticate with the Anthropic API. This is standard and expected behavior for API tooling. The key is used only for direct API calls to Anthropic, not transmitted elsewhere.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is read only for Anthropic SDK initialization - the canonical use pattern for Anthropic API client libraries.

low_findings.title: File Write to User-Specified Path
low_findings.description: The tool writes compressed content back to the same file path the user specifies. A backup is created before overwriting. The path is resolved to an absolute path and validated for existence and size (max 500KB). No files outside the user-specified path are modified.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File write is to user-specified path only with backup creation and size validation - standard pattern for file transformation tools.

content.user_title: Compress memory files to reduce token costs
content.value_statement: Large memory files like CLAUDE.md waste tokens on every AI session start. This skill compresses them by about 46 percent while keeping all technical content intact.
actual_capabilities: Compress markdown files (.md, .txt, .rst) into concise caveman-style prose
Preserve code blocks, URLs, file paths, commands, and technical terms exactly
Create automatic human-readable backups before overwriting original files
Validate output integrity for headings, code blocks, URLs, and bullet structure
Automatically retry and fix validation errors with targeted patches
Detect and skip non-compressible files (code, config, backups)
limitations: Only compresses natural language files, not code or configuration files
Requires Python 3.10 or higher and access to Claude API or CLI
Maximum file size is 500KB per compression run
Consumes API tokens during compression and any fix retry attempts

use_cases.title: Reduce recurring session costs
use_cases.description: Compress your CLAUDE.md project memory file once. The smaller file loads on every session start, saving tokens every time you work with AI.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers using Claude Code daily

use_cases.title: Optimize team documentation
use_cases.description: Compress shared preference files, style guides, and project conventions. Team members get the same instructions with fewer input tokens per session.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams managing shared AI context files

use_cases.title: Condense long-term project notes
use_cases.description: Compress accumulated todo lists, meeting notes, and decision logs into essential facts. Keep all information without the verbose overhead.
use_cases.target_user: Project managers maintaining AI-readable project context

prompt_templates.title: Compress a single file
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress the file at CLAUDE.md into caveman format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick compression of your main project memory file

prompt_templates.title: Compress multiple preference files
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress these memory files: CLAUDE.md, todos.md, and preferences.txt.
prompt_templates.scenario: Batch compress all project context files at once

prompt_templates.title: Recompress after editing
prompt_templates.prompt: I updated CLAUDE.original.md with new coding standards. Compress it again to replace CLAUDE.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: After editing the human-readable backup, regenerate the compressed version

prompt_templates.title: Compress with custom model
prompt_templates.prompt: Set CAVEMAN_MODEL to claude-haiku and compress CLAUDE.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a specific model for compression when you want faster or cheaper processing

output_examples.input: A 706-token CLAUDE.md file with TypeScript preferences, testing guidelines, and project conventions written in full prose.
output_examples.output: A 285-token compressed version preserving all technical instructions in direct caveman-style prose. Headings, code examples, and URLs remain unchanged. Original saved as CLAUDE.original.md.

output_examples.input: A 1145-token project notes file with architecture descriptions, deployment steps, and team conventions.
output_examples.output: A 535-token compressed file. All section headings preserved exactly. Code blocks and URLs intact. Redundant explanations condensed to essential facts. Backup saved as project-notes.original.md.

best_practices: Edit the .original.md backup file, then recompress. This keeps your source of truth in human-readable format.
Run compression after major updates to memory files. Fresh edits often add verbose explanations that compress well.
Review the compressed output before relying on it. Validation checks technical content but style changes may affect nuance.
anti_patterns: Do not compress code files, configuration files, or any file with primarily code content. The tool will skip these automatically.
Do not delete the .original.md backup. It is your human-readable source and recovery point.
Do not run compression repeatedly on unchanged files. Each run consumes API tokens. Compress only after meaningful edits.

faq.question: What happens to my original file?
faq.answer: Your original file is overwritten with the compressed version. Before that happens, a backup is saved with the .original.md extension. You can always read or edit the backup.

faq.question: Can I compress files other than CLAUDE.md?
faq.answer: Yes. Any markdown (.md), plain text (.txt), or reStructuredText (.rst) file works. Extensionless files that contain natural language are also supported. Code and config files are skipped automatically.

faq.question: Does compression lose any technical information?
faq.answer: No. Code blocks, URLs, file paths, commands, and technical terms are preserved exactly. The validation step checks headings, code blocks, URLs, and structure after compression. If validation fails, the tool retries with targeted fixes.

faq.question: What if compression fails or produces bad output?
faq.answer: The tool retries up to 2 times with targeted fixes. If all retries fail, your original file is restored and left untouched. The backup is only kept on successful compression.

faq.question: How many tokens does compression save?
faq.answer: On average, about 46 percent of input tokens are saved. Individual files range from 36 to 60 percent savings depending on how verbose the original text is.

faq.question: Do I need an Anthropic API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, either ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set in your environment, or the claude CLI installed with desktop authentication configured. The skill uses whichever is available.
